questions.question: Why do large shopping malls generally have higher prices than weekly markets?

options.A: They sell only imported goods
options.B: They have higher operating costs like rent and maintenance

questions.explanation: Shopping malls have higher expenses such as rent, air-conditioning, electricity, and staff salaries.

questions.question: Which situation best demonstrates the concept of a chain of markets?

options.A: Farmer sells vegetables directly to family
options.B: Producer sells goods to wholesaler, then retailer, then consumer

questions.explanation: The chain of markets involves several intermediaries moving goods from producers to consumers.

questions.question: Why are neighbourhood shops able to compete with large malls despite limited variety?

options.A: They sell luxury goods
options.B: They offer convenience and personal relationships with customers

questions.explanation: Neighbourhood shops are convenient, often provide credit, and maintain personal relationships with customers.

questions.question: What would most likely happen if wholesalers disappeared from the market chain?

options.A: Retailers would have difficulty obtaining goods in bulk

questions.explanation: Wholesalers act as intermediaries who supply goods in bulk to retailers.

questions.question: Which factor most influences the location of a successful market?

options.A: Distance from forests

questions.explanation: Markets are usually located where there is a large number of potential buyers.

questions.question: Why do weekly market traders often move from one place to another during the week?

options.A: To avoid paying taxes
options.B: To sell goods in different markets on different days

questions.explanation: Traders participate in different weekly markets held on different days.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why goods pass through multiple traders before reaching consumers?

options.A: Each trader adds value through storage, transport, or distribution
options.B: Producers refuse to sell goods directly

questions.explanation: Different traders handle transportation, storage, and distribution of goods.

questions.question: Which market type provides maximum convenience for urgent daily purchases?

options.A: International market

questions.explanation: Neighbourhood shops are located close to homes and offer quick access to daily necessities.

questions.question: Which situation best illustrates the importance of retailers in markets?

options.A: Retailers manufacture goods
options.B: Retailers make goods accessible to consumers in small quantities

questions.explanation: Retailers sell goods directly to consumers in manageable quantities.

questions.question: Why do many consumers still prefer weekly markets despite modern shopping malls?

options.A: Lower prices and possibility of bargaining

questions.explanation: Weekly markets often provide cheaper goods and allow bargaining.

questions.question: Which of the following best represents the role of markets in the economy?

options.A: Connecting producers and consumers

questions.explanation: Markets facilitate exchange between producers and consumers.

questions.question: If a retailer increases prices significantly, what is the most likely consumer reaction?

options.A: Consumers will stop buying goods entirely
options.B: Consumers may shift to other sellers

questions.explanation: Consumers usually switch to other sellers offering lower prices.

questions.question: Which factor makes weekly markets economically beneficial for small traders?

options.A: Low investment and flexible selling locations

questions.explanation: Weekly markets require minimal setup costs and allow traders to move between markets.

questions.question: Which feature of malls attracts customers despite higher prices?

options.A: Comfortable shopping environment and entertainment

questions.explanation: Malls offer comfortable shopping environments and entertainment facilities.

questions.question: What would happen if producers sold directly to consumers without intermediaries?

options.A: Distribution might become more difficult for producers

questions.explanation: Intermediaries help with storage, transportation, and distribution of goods.

questions.question: Why is bargaining common in weekly markets but not in malls?

options.A: Prices in malls are usually fixed
options.B: Customers do not talk to sellers

questions.explanation: Malls typically use fixed pricing systems unlike informal markets.

questions.question: Which role is performed by wholesalers that producers may find difficult?

options.A: Transporting and storing goods in large quantities

questions.explanation: Wholesalers handle bulk storage and distribution.

questions.question: Why are neighbourhood markets important in urban areas?

options.A: They provide quick access to daily necessities

questions.explanation: Neighbourhood markets allow people to quickly buy everyday items.

questions.question: Which factor most affects the price difference between two markets selling similar goods?

options.A: Operational costs

questions.explanation: Higher operating costs such as rent and utilities increase product prices.

questions.question: Which group is primarily responsible for producing goods sold in markets?

options.A: Producers

questions.explanation: Producers manufacture or grow the goods sold in markets.

questions.question: Which situation best illustrates competition in markets?

options.A: Two shops selling similar goods at different prices

questions.explanation: Competition occurs when multiple sellers offer similar products.

questions.question: Which type of market benefits the most from mobility of traders?

options.A: Weekly markets

questions.explanation: Weekly markets rely on traders moving between different market locations.

questions.question: Which stage in the market chain directly interacts with the final buyer?

options.A: Retailer

questions.explanation: Retailers sell goods directly to the final consumer.

questions.question: Why do producers often depend on intermediaries?

options.A: Intermediaries help distribute goods to wider markets

questions.explanation: Intermediaries help producers reach distant markets and customers.

questions.question: Which factor makes markets essential for economic activity?

options.A: They enable exchange of goods and services

questions.explanation: Markets allow buyers and sellers to exchange goods and services, supporting the economy.
